title: TVET Foundations: Competence-Based Education and Work-Life Relevance
purpose: Introduce TVET as competence development connected to real occupational performance and employability.
task: Read a workplace brief and produce a competence map showing knowledge, skill, attitude, tools, safety and evidence for one job role.
scenario: A training centre is launching a solar installation short course. Industry partners say graduates know theory but cannot prepare a safe installation task.
demo: Learner analyses the installation brief, identifies performance criteria, explains required tools and demonstrates a safe preparation sequence using a checklist.

title: Occupational Standards and Task Analysis
purpose: Convert syllabus topics into real work tasks aligned with occupational standards and employer expectations.
topic: Job roles, standards and learning outcomes
task: Break one occupational role into 5 observable tasks, required tools, quality indicators and assessment evidence.
scenario: A hospitality programme teaches customer service as a topic, but hotels want trainees who can handle check-in, complaints and handover records.
demo: Learner creates a task analysis sheet for hotel reception and performs a simulated check-in with a quality checklist.

title: Workshop Safety, Tools, Materials and Quality Culture
purpose: Build practical safety routines, tool discipline and quality checks into every workshop lesson.
task: Set up a safe workstation, select tools/materials, complete risk controls and produce a quality inspection note.
scenario: A carpentry class begins cutting before checking guards, PPE and measurement accuracy, leading to waste and unsafe habits.
demo: Learner performs pre-start safety inspection, sets up the station, completes the task and records quality checks against tolerance criteria.

title: Technical Communication and Workplace Documentation
purpose: Teach learners to document work clearly through job cards, handover notes, diagrams, logs and customer records.
task: Complete a job card, explain the completed task orally and prepare a handover note for the next worker.
scenario: An automotive trainee repairs a fault but leaves no written record, so the next technician repeats diagnostic work.
demo: Learner completes the repair documentation, gives a structured handover and answers follow-up questions from the assessor.

title: Digital Skills and Workplace Technology
purpose: Embed practical digital tools, data entry, device use and ethical technology habits into vocational learning.
task: Use a digital tool to record inventory, update a work order, capture evidence and protect customer data.
scenario: A logistics learner uses paper notes that get lost; the warehouse needs accurate stock updates and traceability.
demo: Learner updates a stock spreadsheet/system, attaches photo evidence, checks data accuracy and explains privacy controls.

title: Work-Based Learning, Apprenticeship and Employer Partnerships
purpose: Design workplace learning with clear roles for learner, teacher, supervisor and employer.
topic: Learning at work and employer partnership
task: Create a workplace learning plan with task schedule, mentor responsibilities, evidence points and feedback routine.
scenario: A student is placed in a workshop but only observes. The employer needs a structured plan with safe progressive tasks.
demo: Learner presents a placement plan and simulates a mentor check-in using evidence from a completed workplace task.

title: Customer Service, Teamwork and Professional Behaviour
purpose: Develop professional conduct, communication, teamwork and customer care as observable workplace behaviours.
task: Handle a customer need, communicate with team members, solve a service problem and record the outcome.
scenario: A salon trainee performs the technical service but does not greet the customer, confirm needs or explain aftercare.
demo: Learner role-plays consultation, confirms the service, collaborates with a teammate and records aftercare advice.

title: Entrepreneurship, Employability and Career Readiness
purpose: Prepare learners to find work, price services, present themselves professionally and understand enterprise basics.
topic: CV writing, job search and enterprise
task: Prepare a job application pack and price a small service using cost, time, value and customer communication.
scenario: A graduate can complete technical work but cannot explain services, quote prices or present skills to employers.
demo: Learner presents a CV/profile, pitches a service package, justifies pricing and responds to employer questions.

title: Green Skills, Sustainability and Resource Efficiency
purpose: Make sustainability visible through waste reduction, energy awareness, material efficiency and responsible work practices.
task: Audit a practical task for waste, energy, safety and environmental impact, then propose an improvement.
scenario: A catering workshop wastes ingredients and disposables, increasing cost and environmental impact.
demo: Learner completes a waste audit, adjusts the workflow and demonstrates the improved process with before/after evidence.

title: Inclusive TVET Support and Individual Learning Pathways
purpose: Support diverse learners through accessible instructions, staged practice, dignity and personalised progression.
task: Adapt one workshop task using visual steps, peer support, safe repetition and alternative evidence collection.
scenario: A learner understands the job orally but struggles with written instructions and falls behind during practical work.
demo: Learner completes the task using accessible support, then explains the process orally and submits photo/video evidence.

title: Competence Demonstration Assessment and Evidence Portfolios
purpose: Assess practical competence through demonstrations, criteria, assessor observation and portfolio evidence.
task: Plan a competence demonstration with criteria, conditions, evidence, assessor roles and feedback decision.
scenario: The course currently ends with a written test only, but employers need proof learners can perform the job safely and independently.
demo: Learner performs a full practical demonstration under realistic conditions and submits an evidence portfolio.

title: Capstone Industry Project and Continuous Improvement
purpose: Bring together technical competence, communication, teamwork, quality, safety and reflection in a real or simulated industry project.
task: Deliver an industry-linked project from brief to product/service, evidence collection, presentation and improvement reflection.
scenario: An employer requests a small solution: a display stand, simple web page, repaired component, service workflow or community product.
demo: Learner plans, completes and presents the project, shows quality evidence, receives feedback and proposes improvement.
